name: Netflix
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Netflix — the streaming entertainment brand whose visual language is the world's most-copied dark-canvas content grid. Canvas is true cinema-black (#141414) — the deepest dark in the streaming category. The signature is Netflix red (#e50914) — a pure red used only on the wordmark, on the play-button overlay, and on the primary 'Sign In' / 'Sign Up' CTA. Type runs the custom Netflix Sans (a precise grotesk with subtle aperture tweaks) at modest scale — the brand voice is 'content is the hero, chrome stays invisible'. The signature visual is the horizontal-scroll row of poster-art tiles: each row is a curated category ('Continue Watching', 'Top 10', 'Because you watched...') of poster-art rectangles, the brand-defining UI pattern in the streaming category.

design_language.motion_signature: Hero auto-plays muted clip — typically 30 seconds of a featured show with slow camera moves and atmospheric music. Poster-art tiles on row-hover lift 1.08x scale with a 200ms ease-out and reveal hover-preview (auto-play 6-second clip). The signature 'TUDUM' sonic mnemonic — the brand's audio motif — plays on app cold-start.

trademark_signals: True cinema-black canvas (#141414) — the deepest dark in the streaming category — never a gray, never a navy
Netflix red (#e50914) — pure red, used only on the wordmark, on play-button overlays, on the 'Sign In' CTA — never as a section background or category divider

Horizontal-scroll poster-art rows — each row is a category title ('Top 10', 'Trending', 'Because you watched...') above a horizontal-scroll strip of poster-art rectangles — the brand-defining UI pattern
Auto-playing hero clip — top-of-app or top-of-page video that runs muted with subtle volume-control affordance — refusing the static-photograph hero every other streaming service uses
Custom Netflix Sans typography — a grotesk with extremely subtle aperture variations that distinguish it from generic Inter / Helvetica
ai_slop_avoided: Don't lighten the canvas. #141414 is the brand-defining dark; even slight gray tinting reads as Hulu / HBO Max / generic streaming
Don't tint the red. Pure #e50914 — any pinkening or muting drops the urgency
Don't substitute the poster-art rows with featured-cards. The horizontal-scroll category-titled row IS the brand's UI signature
Don't pause the hero clip. The auto-playing muted video IS the streaming-hero moment — static images read as off-brand pre-2015 cable network
Don't introduce a secondary chromatic accent. Black + white + red is the trinity; teal/yellow/blue accents read as off-brand
